observations.recommendation:
Make the necessary correcting entries to apply the change in accounting policy in compliance with COA Circular No. 2022-004, following the suggested correcting entries; and
Restate the comparative figures (CY 2023) in the Financial Statements as required in Item 4.6 of COA Circular 2022-004.

In view of the foregoing observations, we reiterated our previous audit recommendation re that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant and the Budget Officer to ensure the proper use of budgetary and expense accounts. This is to avoid the inappropriate charging of expenditures to the OMOE account and to ensure the accurate presentation of specific accounts in the financial statements.
Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting journal entries to reflect the correct balances of the mentioned expenses in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant:
Conduct a comprehensive review and investigation of the accounts reflecting negative balances and implement the necessary correcting journal entries based on the findings; and
Include a disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements regarding the total amount of the misstatement and its potential impact on related accounts.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to.
Submit a request for the write-off of the dormant account following COA Circular No. 93-404.
Maintain subsidiary ledger and conduct regular reconciliation of the accounts to ensure that the balances of reciprocal accounts are equal at all times.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to discontinue the practice of charging and recording prior year’s expenditures to the current year; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the following correcting entry:

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to provide the lacking disclosures and comply with the disclosure requirements of IPSAS and Manual on NGAS for LGUs by adequately inserting a concise narrative on significant changes and breakdown of material balances to ensure that relevant information is available to the intended users of the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer to implement stricter budgetary controls and ensure that disbursements of EME do not exceed the 2% threshold of Basic Real Property Tax (RPT) collections from the preceding year, as prescribed under Section 325(h) of RA 7160.
Additionally, given that the item “Transportation Allowance for CDWs” is recurring and significant, we recommended that the Budget Officer conduct a thorough review to determine the appropriate budget allocation for these expenditures.

We recommended that management revisit the LDRRMFIP, review and prioritize implementable activities, and ensure that available non-QRF funds, particularly those accumulated in the Special Trust Fund, are timely and efficiently utilized in accordance with the DRRM objectives.
We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (MDRRMO) to submit a copy of the updated LDRRMFIP. This will enable the Audit Team to provide more targeted and effective recommendations toward optimizing the utilization of available LDRRMF resources, particularly those held in the Special Trust Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
The Municipal Accountant to implement a standardized checklist of required documents for cash advances as discussed above.
The Municipal Accountant use applicable measures allowed under regulations to enforce timely liquidation of cash advances.
The Municipal Accountant to ensure the proper classification of cash advances to accurately reflect their nature and to facilitate effective monitoring.

Instruct all collecting officers to remit their collections and accomplish RCD at the close of each business day pursuant to Section 29 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.; and
Implement a system to prevent the buildup of unremitted collections by ensuring all collections are properly remitted by the end of each business day. One approach is to establish a cutoff period where collecting officers in the municipal treasury office must submit their collections by 3:00 P.M. This way, they won't struggle to prepare their RCDs by 5:00 P.M. since the workload is lighter. Field collectors should arrive at the office by 4:00 P.M. This protocol will reduce the risk of loss, misapplication, or unauthorized use of public funds.

We have recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly use the PCF for the payment of authorized non-recurring petty operating or miscellaneous expenses that cannot be conveniently paid by check; and
Designate a specific disbursing officer responsible under the Municipal Treasury Office for the payment of financial assistance and its subsequent liquidation.

With the foregoing observations, we recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all Accountable Officers comply with the fidelity bonding requirements as outlined in TC No. 02-2019, dated April 25, 2019. The fidelity bond acts as a safeguard for the agency, providing security for Municipal Government in cases of defalcations, shortages, or unrelieved accountabilities, covering actual losses up to the specified bond amount.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, to undertake the following courses of action, to wit:
Ensure that all supporting documents of disbursement vouchers are complete and duly signed before processing the payment.
Ensure that all financial transaction documents from the Treasury Office are promptly turned over to the Accounting Office for timely recording of transactions. This will contribute to the timely submission of accounts to COA.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Point System to conduct a gender analysis prior to the preparation of the GAD Plan and Budget and ensure the generation and use of sex-disaggregated data to accurately identify and prioritize gender issues. This will help prevent the misclassification of general services as GAD-related activities and promote the implementation of programs that meaningfully address gender disparities, thereby enhancing the impact and compliance of the Municipality’s GAD initiatives.
Additionally, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Point System identify implementable gender-responsive programs or expand existing initiatives to optimize the use of available funds, thereby maximizing the impact of gender and development efforts in the locality.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor take immediate action to establish the Committee on the Revision of Fees and Charges through the issuance of an Executive Order, in strict adherence to the requirements outlined in DILG-DOF Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2019-001. The Committee should be tasked with the following critical functions:
Conduct a thorough assessment of the current fees and charges imposed by the Municipal Government to determine their alignment with prevailing economic conditions and regulatory requirements;
Ensuring that the rates imposed, or to be imposed, accurately reflect the true cost of services rendered by the Municipality, in compliance with the principles of fairness, transparency, and fiscal responsibility; and
Recommend necessary amendments to the Local Revenue Code to recover the full cost of services and align the Municipal Government’s revenue generation capacity with its fiscal needs.
Subsequently, the Sangguniang Bayan should prioritize the swift review and approval of the revised Local Revenue Code to address the Municipality’s evolving financial requirements. The prompt enactment of the amended Code is critical to securing additional revenue streams that will fund vital development projects and further support the Municipality’s long-term growth and welfare.

We have reiterated the previous audit recommendation that management avoid reimbursement as a mode of procurement and adopt public bidding or other alternative modes of procurement provided under RA 9184, to promote transparency and to avoid possible source of irregularities or corruption. Furthermore, the Municipal Accountant must comply with the Revenue Regulations set by the BIR with respect to withholding of taxes to avoid possible tax penalties; and
Lastly, to avoid resorting to reimbursement on emergency purchases, the importance of preparing an Annual Procurement Plan (APP) must be emphasized.   The APP should include quantity and quality of the items to be procured, the mode of procurement, and the timelines for procurement.  Items that were not initially included may still be procured through the preparation of Supplemental APP.

To keep records of cash tickets actually issued to them by the MT; and
To record in their logbooks the range of serial numbers (from# – to#) of the cash tickets sold and those remaining under their custody daily.

Direct the Municipal Accountant that all disbursements be supported with complete documentary requirement as prescribed in COA Circular 2012-001.
Thereafter, exercise prudence in the granting of allowances to its officials and employees and strictly adhere to the existing laws, rules and regulations to avoid disallowance in audit.
Exercise prudence in the utilization of resources to avoid wastage of government funds.

We have recommended the management the following:
For the Accounting office to prepare and maintain subsidiary ledger for each kind of PPE owned by the municipality;
For the management to create an Inventory Committee that will conduct a complete annual physical count of PPE in accordance with Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Vol. I. A representative of the Auditor be invited to witness the inventory taking for this purpose, in the absence of Inventory Committee, the Municipal Treasurer shall take responsibility; and
For the Inventory Committee or Municipal Treasurer and Accounting office to reconcile the differences, if any, between the physical inventory report and the general ledger balance of each PPE account; and subsequently identify if the difference represents PPE that are still existing, no longer existing but with pertinent records, or no longer existing and without pertinent records. The conduct of property disposal shall be coursed thru the guidelines/procedures under COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
The management informed the Audit team during the exit conference that they created Inventory committee to be headed by the OIC-Municipal Accountant to conduct annual physical inventory counting of PPE in order to ascertain the assertions on existence, condition, and valuation. They concur with the recommendations.
non-maintenance of property, plant and equipment ledger cards by Accounting Office and property cards by the Municipal Treasurer
Verification of accounting records showed that the PPE accounts totaling to ₱127,366,688.62 as of December 31, 2020 were not supported with Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC).
Pertinent provisions of NGAS Manual, Vol. I relating to the accountability and responsibility of government officials and employees over property, plant and equipment provide:
Section 120. The Chief Accountant shall maintain [P]roperty, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) for each category for assets; and Real Property Ledger Cards (RPLC) for lands
The PPELC shall record the acquisition, description, custody, estimated life, rate of depreciation, disposal and other information about the PPE, based on the source documents of the transactions.
Likewise, no Property Cards were maintained by the Municipal General Services Office contrary with Section 119 of the Manual on NGAS, Vol. I for LGUs which states that:
Sec. 119. Property Records to be Maintained. – The General Services Officer or the Local Treasurer, as the case maybe, shall number each type of supplies and maintain Stock Cards per stock number. He shall likewise maintain Property Cards per category of property, plant and equipment.
The balance of property cards shall always reconcile with the ledger cards of the accounting office. The absence of these records resulted in difficulty of reconciliation between the results of physical inventory and accounting records. It also led to poor control as items can be lost thru theft without being detected.
We have recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to maintain property, plant and equipment cards reconciled with the property cards of the Municipal Treasurer.
non-renewal of Property Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR)/ Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE)
Verification made by the Audit Team disclosed that the Municipal Treasurer was not able to update/ renew the Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) after issuance contrary to Section 492 of GAAM, Volume I, which states that equipment issued by the property officer for official use of officials and employees shall be covered by ARE, which shall be renewed every January of the 3rd year after issue.
In view of the foregoing, the municipal government property is at risk of loss due to the absence of documents that will point to the determination of accountability over the municipal government’s property.
We have recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the General Services Officer to update/ renew each Property Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR) / Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) every three years after issue or every time there is a change in accountability in accordance with GAAM Volume I.

We have recommended the Management to require the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to discontinue the practice of charging prior year’s expenditure to current year appropriation and that proper matching of expenses against appropriation should always be observed pursuant to the above-cited regulations.

We have recommended and the Management concurred to direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to refer with the description of accounts provided under COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 in recording transactions.

We have recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to trace the discrepancy between the balances of the accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds and effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts through the preparation of Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) duly supported with documents, explanations and/or computations in order to reflect the correct balances of accounts in the financial statements.  Moreover, to conduct regular reconciliation of the accounts to ensure that the balances of reciprocal accounts are the same at all times.

We have recommended and the Management concurred to:
To stop the practice of splitting purchase requisitions or contracts and other related transactions pursuant to COA Circular No. 76-41, to promote effective competition and transparency in the procurement process and obtain a fair and equitable contract for the best interest of the government.
The BAC to deliberate properly on the appropriate mode of procurement to be used, before the Municipality acquires the needed goods, thus avoiding a similar scenario to happen.
To refrain from indicating in the Purchase Request the specific brand name when purchasing equipment, unless pursuant to the exception stated in Section 18 of the IRR of Republic Act No. 9184.

We have recommended and the Head of the Procuring Entity agreed to direct the Bids and Awards Committee to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report and approved by him and submit to the GPPB in compliance with Section 12.2 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We have recommended and the management agreed to prepare the necessary documents to support the veracity of the claims in granting the honoraria/ incentives to the BAC and TWG Members pursuant to COA Circular 2012-001. We further recommend the OIC- Municipal Accountant not to certify the vouchers unless all supporting documents are attached in order to ensure compliance.

We have recommended and the management agreed to comply with the following:
Effect the termination of the contract/ refrain from renewing the contract of the consultants who failed to perform their duties and responsibilities as supported by their monthly accomplishment reports to be submitted to the HRMO of the municipality;
Stop the practice of hiring consultants for tasks which do not require expertise and that can be performed by the regular employees to avoid incurrence of additional expenditure which unfavourable to the agency; and
Require the consultants to submit curriculum vitae and other relevant information that would support their credentials and eligibilities in their chosen field of expertise and for the management to create a databank of the consultants’ credentials/qualifications in order to determine whether their accomplishments are conformable to their qualifications and to justify their appointment as consultants.

To ensure that financial assistance granted is valid, regular and reasonable, we have recommended and the Management agreed:
Formulate policies and guidelines or adopt the policies and guidelines promulgated by DSWD on the grant of financial assistance under the AICS program to strengthen the controls employed in the selection of eligible beneficiaries;
Ensure that the MSWDO strictly assess and evaluate all applicants requesting financial assistance out of the AICS program including the identification of nearest kin who serves as the claimant; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to be more circumspect in reviewing completeness of supporting documents.

We have recommended and the Management agreed to comply to strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 343 of Republic Act No. 7160 and impose control measures to limit the expenses on meals and snacks and save more funds for other legitimate expenditures of the local government unit, if the same cannot be avoided. Also be reminded that the cost of meals and snacks spent for entertaining visitors/ guests of offices concerned be charged to their monthly representation allowance which is the very purpose of such.

We have recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to direct all collecting officers to remit their collections and accomplish their Report of Collections and Deposits (RCD) at close of each business day pursuant to Section 29 of the Manual of New Government Accounting System, Vol. 1 and see to it that no accumulation of cash from collections shall be made by any collector to avoid loss, misapplication and/or unauthorized use.

We have recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to comply to prepare and submit regularly the monthly Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (CRAAF) in order to establish the accountability of accountable forms and facilitate the review and verification of the said report at any given time.

We have recommended and the Municipal Treasure agreed to include checks for all bank accounts maintained by the Municipality in the RAAF and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor as prescribed in Section 59 of the LTOM and Section 50 of the Manual of NGAs to ensure that all checks are properly controlled and monitored as to requisition and issuance. Furthermore, the issuance of checks must be reported chronologically in the RCI that includes cancelled or voided checks. The checks that have been cancelled or voided shall also be disclosed in the RAAF.

We have recommended and the management concurred to:
Properly fill up the salient details needed in preparing Purchase Requests pursuant to the Manual on New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for Local Government Units; and
BAC upon receipt of the PR check and verify the description and specification of the supplies/ goods ordered and if some of the details were not filled up, return the said PR to the requisitioner or concerned office.

We have recommended and the Head of the Procuring Entity agreed to direct the members of Bids and Awards Committee to prepare, accomplish, and submit wholly the salient documentary requirements forming part of the project procurement.

We have recommended the Local Chief Executive to:
Enjoin the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to design and implement projects that are for socio-economic and environmental development that partake the nature of investment or capital expenditure pursuant to the mandate of the DILG DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-01 dated February 22, 2017.
For the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that expenditures involving 20% Development Fund are solely for the purpose of the fund; and for the OIC-Municipal Accountant to verify the same before certifying the disbursement vouchers; and
For the management to prepare a comprehensive Annual Investment Plan (AIP) encompassing the municipality’s priority development projects out of 20% DF, in accordance with DILG DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-01.
During the exit conference, the management stated that the expenses in question still partake social, economic, and environmental development activities despite that those are not capital investment in nature. Also, the construction materials for repair given to schools are also part of social development.
The Audit Team sustained the observations. The DILG DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-01 is particular on the specific expenditures that the fund may be utilized. Moreover, the agency could have charged against the Special Education Fund the payment for materials given to schools.

We have recommended and the Management agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to render their accounts and submit the same for audit in accordance with Section 347 of Republic Act No. 7160 and Item 7.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009. This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension/ Disallowance as a result of post-audit.

We have recommended that management formulate an action plan to implement the audit recommendations, and submit immediately the duly accomplished AAPSI form to the Audit Team, copy furnished the other oversight government bodies pursuant to Section 99 of Republic Act No. 11260.

We have recommended and the management agreed to:
Economically, efficiently and effectively utilize the resources of the Municipality by discouraging and preventing unnecessary and extravagant expenditures; and
Purchase essential items needed in the day to day operations of their respective municipal offices to prevent wastage of government funds.

We have recommended and Management agreed to refrain from reimbursement and strictly comply with the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) concerning Government procurement by meticulously planning all procurements thru the preparation of Annual Procurement Plan (APP) and make revisions as necessary, to ensure that the government and the people get maximum benefits and quality of service from the contracts. Compliance to the requirements of public bidding calls for transparency of government transactions and accountability of public officers.

observations.recommendation:
1.6 We recommended that, henceforth, Management exerts all necessary
efforts to render the agency’s accounts within the prescribed period.

2.7 We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer prepares, maintains
and submits to COA the different Registries of Appropriations, Allotments
and Obligations as prescribed under the Manual on the New Government
Accounting System for Local Government Units, for reconciliation with the
registries of the Accounting Unit as well as for audit/verification of the
veracity of the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts.
Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer
maintain the four registries as required in Section 4(h) of the Manual on
NGAS to monitor appropriations, allotments received and obligations
incurred against said allotment and in order to prevent incurrence of
obligations in excess of allotments received or approved appropriations.

3.6 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensures that fund
transfers are taken up properly in the books of accounts, utilized solely for
its intended purpose, and properly reported. Fund transfers to LGUs
should be debited to Due from LGUs account and will only be credited
upon submission of liquidation documents by the LGU recipient. The
proposed adjusting entry is as follows:
Due from LGUs 100,000.00
Transfers for Project Equity Share/LGU Counterpart 100,000.00

3.7 We also recommended for the submission of the Memorandum of
Agreement/ Trust Agreement, approved Program of Work, certification by
the Municipal Accountant that funds previously transferred, if any, have
been liquidated and the Sangguniang Bayan authorization for the
Municipal Mayor to enter into agreement and/or contract.

4.4 We recommended that Management, henceforth, requires concerned
officials to promptly submit copies of government contracts and their
supporting documents within the prescribed period to enable the audit
team timely review of transactions.

5.5 We recommended that Management submits complete supporting
documents as provided in COA Circular No. 2012-001 depending on the
nature of payment to prove the propriety and legality of the claims.
Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant see to it that succeeding transactions
be properly supported with complete documentation and in accordance
with pertinent laws, rules and regulations in order to establish the validity
and propriety of the transactions and disbursements. Processing and
payment of claims that are not properly documented shall be discontinued.

6.7 We reiterated our recommendation in the prior years that the Municipality
submits a copy of the approved APP supported with the PPMP and changes
to the APP, if any, for CY 2019 and henceforth, an APP be prepared,
approved and submitted to the GPPB.

7.10 We recommended that Management submits the above-stated performance
security, and written justification/s on the performance securities with
alteration/tamper. The alteration/ tampering is highly discouraged and
should be discontinued. Henceforth, future winning bidders comply with
all the requirements set forth under the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184
such as the posting of valid performance security before the awarding of
contract and contract signing.

7.11 We also recommended that henceforth, the TWG and BAC further validate
posted performance securities from the Insurance Commission to ensure
that they are project specific before the awarding of contract and contract
signing.

8.5 We recommended that Management submits written justification/s on the
above noted deficiencies. Henceforth, future payments on infrastructure
projects must be in strict compliance with the provisions of Annex E of the
Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

8.6 We also recommended that Management submits the copies of Certificates
of Final Acceptance and the irrevocable standby letters of credit, bank
guarantees and/or surety bonds as substitute for the release of retention
moneys, for validation/audit.

9.9 In view of the above noted observations, we recommended to Management
the following:

9.9.1 Submit a copy of the duly approved Local School Board Budget
presented by program, activity and project (P/A/P), by allotment
class and per school, and the SEF utilization report in accordance
with DepED-DBM-DILG JC No. 1 s. 2017;
9.9.2 Submit the above noted lacking supporting documents in order to
determine the validity and propriety of the charges made to the
fund; and
9.9.3 Henceforth, the LSB Budget and SEF Fund Utilization Report be
submitted and made public together with the Annual Budget of the
LGU and that charges to be made against the fund should be
properly supported with the required certification and documentary
requirements.

10.6 We reiterated our recommendations that Management:

10.6.1 Submits to the audit team copies of the Memorandum of Agreement,
SB Resolution and Program of Work or Project Proposal for the
above-mentioned fund received and its utilization for proper
evaluation of the project; and
10.6.2 Henceforth, all transactions involving fund transfer from other
government agencies should be fully documented in accordance with
the COA Circular No. 2012-001.

11.3 We recommended that Management complies strictly with the provisions of
DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 and the related subsequent provisions on 20%
Development Fund investment planning and utilization. Henceforth,
Management submit the duly approved 20% Development Fund AIP, for
audit/evaluation.

12.2.7 We recommended that Management submits written justification/s
and the documentary requirements for the above-noted lapses.
Henceforth, all procurement of the Municipality be conducted
through public bidding as mandated under R.A. No. 9184 and its
IRR, unless justified by the conditions provided under Rule XVI of
the IRR, and as recommended by the BAC and duly approved by
the head of the procuring entity. Alternative methods of
procurement shall be resorted to only in highly exceptional cases
provided for in R.A. No. 9184. In all instances, the procuring entity
shall ensure that the most advantageous price for the LGU is
obtained.

For the Accounting office to prepare and maintain subsidiary ledger for each kind of PPE owned by the municipality;
Management create an Inventory Committee that will conduct a complete annual physical count of PPE in accordance with Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Vol. I. A representative of the Auditor be invited to witness the inventory taking for this purpose; and
For the Treasury and Accounting office to coordinate and reconcile the differences between the physical inventory report and the general ledger balance of each PPE account; which will then be the basis of the Municipal Accountant to make appropriate adjustments to reflect the correct balances of each PPE account.
We have recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to maintain property, plant and equipment cards reconciled with the property cards of the Municipal Treasurer.
We have recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the General Services Officer to update/ renew each Property Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR) / Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) every three years after issue or every time there is a change in accountability in accordance with GAAM Volume I.

We have recommended and Management agreed that the accounting and reporting guidelines on the Local Roads Asset Management System prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015 be strictly complied. Thus, require the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network. Compliance to the requirements of public bidding calls for transparency of government transactions and accountability of public officers.

We have recommended and Management agreed to observe prudence in spending government funds and strictly comply with the provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2011-59 dated April 25, 2011 so as not to incur extravagant expenditures as cited in COA Circular 2012-003.

We have recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the Municipal Officials and Employees to observe the provisions stated in COA Circular 2012-003 to avoid incurrence of excessive, extravagant and unnecessary expenditures.
Further, to refund the amount of P21,000.00 representing the excess reimbursement for food expenses during the Market Day Christmas Celebration.

We have recommended and the Management agreed to strengthen the internal control system with regard to monitoring of gasoline/ fuel expenses to ensure that only the government-owned vehicles charge such expenses.
Further, the management to stop allowing the reimbursement/ payment of gasoline/ fuel expenses to private vehicles as such is considered illegal as provided for under COA Circular 2012-003 pursuant to COA Circular 96-004, to avoid suspension/ disallowance in audit.

We have recommended and the Management agreed to immediately stop the granting of incentives to Job Order personnel as it has no legal basis in order to avoid the issuance of the Notice of Disallowance.

We have recommended and the Management agreed to immediately stop granting monthly transportation allowances and per diems to the members of Child Development Workers (CWDs), Barangay Nutrition Scholars (BNSs), and Federation of Senior Citizens (FSCAP) as these do not have legal basis, and not in conformity with the provisions of Local Budget Circular No. 102 dated January 17, 2013 and Executive Order No. 77 prescribing the guidelines in the grant of Transportation allowance and per diems respectively.

We further recommend the management to refrain from granting additional claims on meals and accommodations incurred during seminars/ trainings and other similar events if these were already included in the registration fees paid to the organizing agencies to avoid double payment.

We have recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to utilize the scare resources of the Municipality effectively and efficiently. Further, to send officials and/or employees to seminar and training that could truly enhance their skills and/or knowledge in performing their official mandate/ function.

We have recommended and the Management agreed to preclude Municipal Officials assigned with government service vehicles from receiving Transportation Allowance (TA) in compliance with the provisions of Item 8.2.1 of Local Budget Circular No. 103, to avoid suspension/ disallowance in audit.
We have recommended and that Accounting Office agreed to reconstruct the maintenance/ repair record of each equipment/ vehicle owned and/or operated by the Municipal Government.

We have recommended and the Management agreed to strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 343 of Republic Act No. 7160 and impose control measures to limit the expenses on meals and snacks and save more funds for other legitimate expenditures of the local government unit.

We have recommended and the Management agreed to utilize the LDRRM Fund solely for projects and activities that address the thematic areas: disaster prevention and mitigation, disaster preparedness, disaster response, and disaster rehabilitation and recovery, as provided under Section 5.0 of  NDRRMC_DBM_DILG Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 in order to strengthen the capacity of the municipality and its constituents to be a disaster-resilient community, reduce disaster risks, and enhance disaster preparedness and response capability.

Enjoin the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to design and implement projects that are for socio-economic and environmental development that partake the nature of investment or capital expenditure pursuant to the mandate of the DILG DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-01 dated February 22, 2017; and
For the Municipal Budget Officer and Accountant not to allow the charging of expense items that are not within the allowable expenses as listed in Sections 2.0 and 3.0 of the same circular; and
For the management to maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund allocated for the implementation of various projects, programs and activities in achieving the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the LGU.

We have recommended and the GAD Focal Person agreed to prepare a comprehensive GAD Plan and Budget, and for the management to allocate at least 5% of the total budget appropriation for the implementation of GAD-related activities in accordance with Section 36 of Republic Act No. 7910.

In view of the foregoing, it is recommended and management agreed to strictly adhere with the cited provisions of Manual on New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for Local Government Units, and to device strong internal control to safeguard the scare resources of the Municipality.

We have reiterated the previous recommendation and the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer, together with the accountable officers concerned agreed to strictly comply with guidelines requiring the timely preparation of reports and submission of accounts. This in effect would facilitate a more holistic review and evaluation of contracts.

Subsequently, a report on RPT delinquencies must be prepared by the Treasury office and impose penalties and avail remedies set by laws to compel the delinquent taxpayers to pay their taxes on time.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the agency submit a copy of Annual Procurement Plan (APP) as well as PMR of the LGU for CY 2017 and henceforth, an APP and PMR should be prepared, approved and submitted to the GPPB.
observations.reason: APP and PMR for CY 2017 were not submitted; reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: In view of the above noted deficiencies, we have recommended that:  Management identify the mode of procurement they used in the hiring of consultant;  The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer justify the payment of the transactions despite the lack of appropriation and absence of the BAC Resolution recommending the mode of procurement used and award of the contract;  The Municipal Mayor justify entering into contract without the concurrence of the majority of the BAC, lack of appropriation for the said purpose and absence of prior authorization from the Sangguniang Bayan;  Management clarify the terms of reference of all the contracts entered into by the LGU. Justify the increase in contract cost and extension of the contract; and  Management require the immediate submission of accomplishment reports by the hired consultants.
observations.reason: Required documents and justifications for consultancy procurements were not submitted; training awaited from DSWD for CLJIP and other required actions were not accomplished.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses. Submit the LCPC Annual Work and Financial Plan, CLJIP as well as SB resolution adopting the specific expenditures allowed from the said fund, if any. Henceforth, management abide with the rules and regulations with regard to the formulation of budget and utilization of the 1% LCPC fund.
observations.managementAction: The CLJIP is not yet crafted, and management is waiting for training from the DSWD.
observations.reason: CLJIP not crafted; training awaited from DSWD; required plans and resolutions not submitted.

observations.recommendation: In view of the noted lapses, we have recommended the following:  The Municipal Mayor create the required Gender Responsive Monitoring and Evaluation (M&E) System;  The MSWDO being the leading authority in identifying gender related issues and concerns, see to it that GAD activities are identified through the use of HGDG and GAD Database, that the GAD plan are regularly updated and that the identified projects do address the GAD issues and concern in the long run; and  Henceforth, management should follow the prescribed format of GAD plan and see to it that said plan and budget are approved by the DILG and the PPDO.
observations.reason: GAD M&E system not created; GAD Code not adapted; DILG-approved GPB not submitted; actions reiterated in current report.

2.9 We have recommended that Management –
2.9.1 Submit supporting documents as enumerated in Item 2.6;
2.9.2 Stop the monthly payment of honoraria to national government
officials until the SB pass resolution authorizing the grant of and
specifying the rates of additional allowances to these officials, subject

We have reiterated that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Inventory Committee to complete the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant, and Equipment (RPCPPE which shall cover all municipal properties including those acquired during prior years. To facilitate this, we have further recommended the Accounting Department to be required to maintain property ledger cards for each category of property, plant, and equipment to record deliveries of properties. It is further recommended that the necessary RCPPE be prepared and submitted to requiring departments/ offices.

We have recommended that the accounting and reporting guidelines on the Local Roads Asset Management System prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015 be strictly complied. Thus, require the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network. Likewise, require the Municipal Treasurer to maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map, and keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components. Moreover, create an inventory committee to conduct the physical count and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System.

We have recommended the management to adopt public bidding, or other alternative methods of procurement as the case may be, pursuant to the provisions of the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184 to enable transparency and competitiveness in government procurement thus availing the most advantageous price possible. It was further recommended that only when the items to be purchased would qualify as those which are included in Section 52.1 and 53.2 of the same IRR, can the LGU issue Certificate of Emergency Purchase.

We have recommended the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Head of the Requesting Unit to prepare disbursement vouchers and their supporting documents to attest the propriety and validity of their transactions. Also, for the Head of the Accounting Unit to affix her signature to the DVs after checking the completeness of the supporting documents, and for the Municipal Treasurer to release the cash/check for payment only after the accomplishment of the DVs and their supporting documents.

We have recommended the management to furnish the Auditors copies of contracts together with all the supporting documents to the COA within five (5) working days from execution thereof pursuant to COA Circular No 87-278 and COA Memorandum No. 2005-027 as reiterated in COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009. This in effect would facilitate a more holistic review and evaluation of contracts.

We have recommended that the management ensure that travelling expenses are made within the prescribed limits in accordance with EO 298. We have further recommended the employees to declare the actual time of departure and arrival from and to their official station in their itinerary of travel, and to the management not to allow employees from attending seminars that they are not supposed to attend based from the letter of invitation from organizing agency. Refund of the amount paid in excess totaling P77,840.00 is hereby recommended, otherwise, a Notice of Disallowance will be issued.

We have reiterated that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer, together with the officers concerned, to exert utmost effort to meet the demands of timely reporting and submission of accounts.  This is to avoid undue delays in the conduct of post audit and preparing the required reports in compliance with existing laws, rules and regulations.

We have recommended the management to submit the copies of approved Purchase Requests, Purchase Orders and Contracts to the Audit Team within the period prescribed in Section 4 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009. Strict compliance as to the prescribed period relative to the submission of monthly reports is to be observed.

We have recommended the management to ensure compliance with the Budget Circular No. 2004-5A of Department of Budget and Management dated October 7, 2005 in granting the honoraria/ incentives to the BAC and TWG Members, and prepare the necessary documents to support the veracity of the claims. Also, we have recommended the OIC- Municipal Accountant not to approve the vouchers unless all supporting documents are attached and to ensure that the amount of the honoraria /incentives do not exceed the threshold set by the same Budget Circular.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant transfer the unspent portion of the LDRRMF from General Fund to Trust Fund pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-02. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant shall record the transfer of fund under Trust Fund using the account “Trust Liabilities-DRRM” as prescribed by PPSAS.

To address the issue on the non-reconciliation of balances of RPT/ SET, we have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare and furnish to the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list showing assessment roll of landowners which will be the basis for the amount due to be recognized as RPT Receivable during the year, pursuant to Section 248 of the Local Government Code.

We have recommended the OIC-Municipal Accountant to exercise care and diligence in recording and classifying the financial transactions so that the financial statements will not be misleading. In addition, she has to prepare necessary adjusting journal entry to present fairly the correct balances of the accounts.

We have reiterated our previous year recommendation for the Municipal Mayor to stop from reimbursing monthly subscription from Globe and Smart aside from the monthly load allowance received by him.  The Local Chief Executive shall choose either to claim for load allowance or to reimburse its actual bill from Globe and Smart, otherwise any excessive claim will be disallowed in audit. It is further recommended that the amount P32,038.00 be refunded, otherwise a Notice of Disallowance will be issued.

We have recommended the management:
To maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund allocated for the implementation of various projects, programs and activities in achieving the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the LGU in compliance with Section 5.0 of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017; and
For the Municipal Budget Officer and Accountant not to allow the charging of expense items that are not within the allowable expenses as listed in Sections 2.0 and 3.0 of the same circular.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer require all Collecting officers  to remit their collections and accomplish their Report of Collections and Deposits (RCD) at close of each business day pursuant to Section 29 of the Manual of New Government Accounting System, Vol. 1 and see to it that no accumulation of cash from collections shall be made by any collector to avoid loss, misapplication and/or unauthorized use.

We have recommended that the accountable officers adhere strictly to the provisions of Sec. 73 of Government Accounting and Auditing Manual Volume I and Section 101 of PD 1445 to avoid the possible fraudulent use of accountable forms and ensure that all accountable forms in their custody are kept diligently.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer enjoin all designated Accountable Officers to adhere to the bonding requirement as provided under Section 101 of PD 1445 and Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 dated August 6, 2009. The fidelity bond serves as a security of Accountable Officers to the agency in case of defalcations, shortages, and unrelieved accountabilities of the actual loss but not exceeding the amount of bond.

We have recommended the Municipal Treasurer and other officials concerned to adopt strategies to improve the collection efficiency and must exert extra effort to have efficient and aggressive tax collection. Since the barangays are receiving their shares from the real property tax, their assistance may be sought in the implementation of the municipality’s tax campaign program, particularly in the dissemination of notices of delinquency to the delinquent real property owners within their jurisdiction.

We have recommended that in order to establish an accurate and reliable balance of all municipal properties recorded in the books, management should undertake the following measures:
 The Municipal Engineer and the Property Custodian conduct a Report of yearly physical count of local road network of Local Road projects and consequently update the local road map of the LGU;
 The Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian the description and cost segregation of road components of various road infrastructure projects; and
 The Municipal Accountant keeps and maintain subsidiary ledger for roads and its components and reconcile the same to the result of the physical at the end of each year.
observations.managementAction: Management Partially implemented.
observations.reason: submitted Report of Physical Count for CY 2018 was unsubmitted. It was observed that the Report of Physical Count of Local Road Network does not include “road lot” components.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the following measures be undertaken by management:
 The Municipal Mayor instructs the Accountant and Treasurer to enforce the collection of the Loans Receivable.
 The Municipal Assessor furnishes the Accountant copy of certified list of taxpayers at the beginning of the year as basis for recording RPT and SET Receivable.
 The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer maintain data as to the composition of the uncollected RPT and SET receivables. Issue demand letter if necessary. A taxpayers. Annual photocopy of reconciliation is to be made between the demand letters issued for tax billing was provided to the audit team for reference.
 Management is advised to exert effort in locating documents pertaining to the receivable accounts. In the event that the supporting documents could no longer be retrieved, a request for write-off could be considered.
observations.managementAction: Management implemented. Loans receivable amounting to P14,000.00 has already been collected as per OR No. 5318563 dated February 3, 2020. The Municipal Assessor furnished copy of certified list of taxpayers. The Municipal Accountant said that demand letter was issued to notify the owners to pay the RPT and SET. Annual reconciliation is to be made between the balances of the RPT/SET Receivables and the subsidiary ledgers. Management is advised to continuously coordinate with the GSIS to ascertain actual amount of liabilities recorded in the financial statements. Some payments made to the GSIS for CY 2018 do not reconcile with the alphalist to the DV and the official receipt. Management should continuously update billing records to ensure that payables were withholdings and the same as payments are made.
observations.reason: The Loans receivable amounting to P14,000.00 was collected as per OR No. 5318563 dated February 3, 2020. Management provided some actions but pertinent documents for other dormant receivables could not be located; certified true copy of schedule of dormant accounts by the Municipal Accountant and approval by the Municipal Mayor together with certified relevant documents were not submitted.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the noted discrepancies between the records of the LGU and other government agencies, we have recommended the following:
 The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer reconcile their record with the GSIS and retrace transaction from previous year to ascertain the validity of the recorded Other Payables account if possible; and
 Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer regularly reconcile their quarterly financial records with the records of other government agencies such as HDMF and GSIS.
observations.managementAction: Management Partially implemented. Some payments made to the GSIS for CY 2018 were done to ensure that payables were the same as billing of the agencies concerned. Reconciliations were done to ensure that withholdings and payments are made.
observations.reason: The LGU continuously coordinates with GSIS to ascertain amounts; however, full reconciliation and supporting documents to fully validate all dormant balances were not submitted.

observations.recommendation: In view of the above noted observation, we have recommended that management submit justification on the reimbursements made by the LGU officials and submit additional supporting documents, if any. Henceforth, limit using reimbursement as method of payment for procurement of property, plant and equipment and MOOE unless there are unforeseen events which will justify the immediate payment thereof.
observations.managementAction: Management said that some of the reimbursements were for travelling and business like trainings and seminars. Management will limit use of reimbursement as method of payment and shall ensure adequate documentation.
observations.reason: Some reimbursements were justified as travel/training expenses; management indicated measures to limit reimbursements but full documentary support for all reimbursements was not provided.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor hire qualified personnel for the mandatory positions as provided under R.A. No. 7160 in order to improve the conduct of LGU operations.
observations.managementAction: Management said that continuous publications were made but no one qualifies for the mandatory positions due to required number of years experience related for the job and additional licensing requirements.
observations.reason: Management continuously published vacancies but applicants did not meet mandatory qualifications; positions remain unfilled.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses. The Municipal Mayor instructs the MPDC to discuss with the LDC the preparation and adoption of both plans for CY 2018 onwards.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted letter informing the municipality that the CLUP 2016 – 2025 of the Municipality has been approved by the SP during their 49th Regular Session on July 3, 2018, and SB Resolution No. 44 series of 2017 adopting the CDP and LDIP of the Municipality for CYs 2017 – 2019.
observations.reason: CLUP and CDP reportedly approved; however completeness and linkage to AIP require further validation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management justify the non-payment of the terminal leave benefits of the former employees. Henceforth, the management is hereby advised to prioritize the payment of terminal leave benefits of the employees for them to enjoy their rightful benefits.
observations.managementAction: Management made partial payments to said personnel.
observations.reason: Partial payments were made; outstanding balance remained as of audit.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses and immediately submit the copies of the contracts for auditorial review and evaluation. Henceforth, adhere to the transparency provisions as provided by the different rules and regulations cited above.
observations.managementAction: Management said that they encountered problems in the posting of the Notices of Award and Proceed due to poor internet connection. They said that the posting of Notices of Award and Proceed in the PhilGEPS website required strong internet connection.
observations.reason: Notices not fully posted on PhilGEPS; posting difficulties cited due to poor internet.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee justify the noted lapses and submit the needed documents for verification. Henceforth, comply with the rules and regulations as required by R.A. No. 9184.
observations.reason: Some procurement deficiencies were addressed but other requirements and supporting documents remained outstanding; reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management justify the noted lapses. Further, we recommend that Management submit the urban/sub-urban greening plan of the Municipality. Henceforth, conduct coordinated tree planting activities and likewise fully utilize the nurseries so as to sustain the NGP project of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Management provided that some benefit were no longer included in the AIP 20% Development Fund for CY 2018. The Municipal Mayor and LDC to monitor compliance. Management is yet to submit documents on second-hand vehicle purchase and registration papers.
observations.reason: Implementation issues persist; some documentation missing and technical matters (e.g., lot availability) affect full compliance.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the Local School Board submit a copy of a duly approved Local School Board Budget as well as justification as to the reason for the failure to submit said budget on time despite various verbal and written demand made; Submit the above noted lacking supporting documents in order to determine the validity of the charges made to the fund; and Henceforth, the LSB Budget should be submitted and made public together with the Annual Budget of the LGU and that charges to be made against the fund should be properly supported with the required certification and documentary requirements.
observations.managementAction: The Local School Board Budget submitted lacks quorum as reiterated in this report. Management committed to submit the duly approved Municipal School Board Budget.
observations.reason: LSB Budget lacked required approvals/quorum; supporting documents were still lacking though management committed to submit approved budget.

observations.recommendation: In view of the above noted lapses, we have presented and recommended the following:  Submit copy of the duly approved SWM Plan from the Solid Waste Management Commission;  Justify the noted lapses primarily the non-establishment of a non-sanitary landfill which will serve as the final disposal sites;  The MPDC sees to it that henceforth all relevant cost for the implementation of the SWMP is integrated to the Annual Budget as well as Annual Investment Plan. Coordination among the different offices is hereby advised;  The Municipal Mayor convenes the SWMB and seeks justification as to the reason for the low success rate for the implementation of SWM projects; and  The members of the SWMB revisit the provisions of the IRR of R.A. No. 9003. Henceforth, implement quality assurance/ monitoring mechanism to facilitate an efficient and effective implementation of the programs indicated in the SWMP.
observations.managementAction: Management will provide copy to the Audit Team of its revised Ten-Year Solid Waste Management Plan 2018 – 2027 duly approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission per NSWMC Resolution No. 599 series of 2019. Accomplishment Report for the activities undergone, if any, for CY 2018 is still for submission.
observations.reason: Revised SWMP to be provided; some actions pending such as securing lot for sanitary landfill and integration of costs into AIP.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management make representation with the Bureau of Treasury and/or the Department of Budget and Management as the case maybe to secure a copy of the SARO/NADAI so as to ascertain the purposes of the funds downloaded to the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted certified true copy of Letter to DBM attached therewith are the Approved Sangguniang Bayan Resolutions, Supplemental Annual Investment Plan (FY 2017-2018 Implementation), Template, Mechanism, Period of Implementation, Projected Number of Beneficiary, and Approved Municipal Development Council, duly received by the DBM on November 21, 2017.
observations.reason: Documents submitted but did not include specific projects for which the fund is to be utilized; further details were not available.

observations.recommendation: In view of the above noted lapses, we have recommended the following actions be undertaken by management:  The Municipal Mayor instructs the Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to justify the above noted observations.  Observe the doctrine of separation of power between the Executive and Legislative department of the LGU. Henceforth, the respective members of the Sangguniang Bayan should only perform functions as prescribed under Section 445, Article II and 446, Article III of R.A. 7160.  Revisit DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 on the expenditure items not covered or are prohibited to be paid out of the 20% Development Fund for guidance. Henceforth, the Municipal Mayor and the members of the LDC monitor compliance with the above cited regulations.  Justify the purchase of second-hand vehicles through a supplier with doubtful qualification and without conducting technical study. Moreover, provide the original documents covering the purchase thereof including the computation of its Residual Useful Life. The submission of the official receipts/ certificate of registration is also hereby asked to be provided by management. Lastly, submit the registration papers of the said vehicles in the name of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Projects with permanent benefit were no longer included in the AIP 20% Development Fund for CY 2018. Management will monitor and follow regulations. Regarding second-hand vehicles, management is yet to submit copy of the SB Resolution for the purchase of second-hand dump trucks, copy of the original documents covering the purchase, documents or certifications on the computation of miles and remaining Residual Useful Life, and registration papers of the said vehicles in the name of the municipality.
observations.reason: Some projects implemented; documentation for second-hand vehicle purchases not yet provided.

observations.recommendation: In view of the foregoing observations, we have recommended that the following actions be undertaken:  The MDRRMO conduct the necessary VAA and formulate a LCCAP in accordance with Book 1 and 2 of the LCCAP Formulation to be integrated to the LDRRMP and AIP of the LGU;  Integrate the result of hazard mapping in the DRRM Plan of the LGU;  The MDRRMO Designate/MSWDO or other personnel in-charge of the distribution of relief goods submit the required liquidation documents for audit purposes;  The Municipal Budget Officer exercise due diligence in the computation of the mandatory trust liabilities of the LGU;  The Municipal Accountant incorporates in the Notes to Financial Statements all required information pertaining to the LDRRM Fund.
observations.managementAction: Management committed to conduct VAA and prepare LCCAP, integrate hazard mapping, designate personnel to submit liquidation documents, exercise diligence in computation of trust liabilities and incorporate required disclosures in the notes.
observations.reason: Some actions taken; VAA and full LCCAP conformity pending; relief goods liquidation documents not fully submitted; required disclosures partly incorporated.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses. The Municipal Engineer is advised to abide with the provisions of DPWH Department Order No. 11 S. 2014.
observations.managementAction: According to management, the ideal design standard for FMR could not be achieved since the road right of way allowed by the constituents were also limited. Enforcing the ideal standard would jeopardize the farms/crops of the tenants which is their primary source of income.
observations.reason: Design standards constrained by right-of-way limitations and livelihood concerns; full DPWH standards not applied.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Inventory Committee to complete physical inventory which shall covers all municipal properties regardless of date acquired so that the Physical Inventory Report submitted to the Office of the Auditor is accurate and reliable as to whereabouts and conditions of all municipal properties.
observations.managementAction: The Treasury unit has provided for the physical inventory report for PPE acquired during the year 2018. But no report was made for those acquired from the previous years. Thus, the audit recommendations were still not implemented.
observations.reason:
The Treasury unit has provided the report of physical inventory count for acquired assets for the year 2018. However, no report was made for those acquired for the year 2017 and beyond. 

This audit observation was reiterated in Finding No. 1, page 28 of this Annual Audit Report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide depreciation expense and impairment loss on the following PPE mentioned above in order to present correctly their balances in the financial statements in consonance with PPSAS.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant provided  depreciation and impairment to Property, Plant and Equipment for the year 2018.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Petty Cash Custodian refrain from performing collecting functions. Further, the Municipal Treasurer to designate a separate employee to handle the collections to ensure that a sound system of internal control is in place in the Treasury Office.
observations.reason:
Implemented

Management already comply with the audit team’s recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that all Revenue Collection Clerks should remit their daily collections to Municipal Treasurer as required under Section 29 of the Manual of NGAS Vol. I. Likewise the Municipal Treasurer should set control measures to avoid or minimize delayed remittance of collections and institute disciplinary actions to concerned accountable employees under her supervision, if necessary.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer has enjoined her collectors to comply with the recommendations, however, some still incur delay in remitting their collections.
observations.reason: Several revenue collecting officers were still delayed in the turnover of their daily collections hence reiterated in Finding No. 18, Page 60 of this Audit Report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Management strictly follow the rules stated in Section 4.1.1 to 4.1.4 of COA Circular 97-002 governing the granting and utilization of cash advance and make sure that all cash advances: (1) Must have Legal Purpose;(2) Previous ones are fully liquidated or settled before another cash advance is granted; (3) Only cash advance for traveling expenses is allowed to Elected Officials, they are not allowed to cash advance for the purpose of procuring equipment and to pay operating expenses of the municipality.
observations.reason:
While there was no substantial refund noted in the cashbooks of the Treasurer, still there were cash advances noted without legal specific purpose, and additional cash advances were still allowed without first liquidating previous cash advances, thus the audit recommendations for these observations were not implemented. 

Reiterated in Finding No. 4, Page 34 of this Annual Audit Report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and accountant to strictly comply with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 and refrain from those expense items that are not connected with the implementation of development projects, programs and activities of the municipality. The 20% Development Fund shall be appropriated and disbursed only for the very purpose for which the fund was established.
observations.managementAction: The management still charged expenses that were not investment in nature to the 20% Development Fund.
observations.reason:
The report on the utilization of 20% Development Fund revealed that a total of P969,624.33 expenses which were not capitalizable were charged against the fund. 

This audit observation reiterated in Finding No. 17.b, Page 59 of this Annual Audit Report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and Municipal Engineer to properly evaluate the technical viability of the projects included in the list of Local Development Projects and to regularly monitor the status of project implementation so that necessary interventions can be introduced at the earliest time possible time to avoid delays and backlogs. Likewise, the BAC members shall schedule early the procurement activities pertaining to development projects to ensure their timely implementation and completion.
observations.managementAction: The management has not implemented the audit recommendations with regard to this observation because they gave more priorities to implement the projects funded by National Agencies.
observations.reason:
The report on the utilization of 20% Development Fund revealed that a total of P12,703,818.42, which represents eleven (11) projects remained unimplemented as of the year-end. 

This audit observation reiterated in Finding No. 17.a, Page 58 of this Annual Audit Report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and Accountant Refrain from utilizing appropriated funds under MOOE in the payment of monetization of Leave Credits. The funds to be utilized in the Payment of monetization of Leave Credits.  The funds to be utilized in the payment of monetization must come from the appropriation of Terminal Leave/Monetization under Ps. Likewise, the Municipal Budget Officer shall appropriate funds for TLB equivalent to the total earn leave credits of all regular officials and employees of the Municipality.
observations.reason: The post-audit of agency’s transactions with regard to the monetization of leave credits revealed that the management has implemented the recommendations as regards this observation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Management strictly remit the compulsory premiums on the period stipulated by the above mentioned-law. The remittances should take priority over payment of any and all other obligations, except salaries and wages of employees.
observations.managementAction: The management has implemented the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: The post-audit of agency’s remittances revealed that the management has implemented the recommendations as regards this observation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly review the disbursement vouchers relating to the payment of Gasoline Expenses and not to allow those issued Drivers Trip Tickets that were utilized for private purposes. Likewise, the person in charge in the issuance of Drivers Trip Ticket shall scrutinize the intention of those official and employees asking for Gas Slip and shall be supported by a Travel Order duly signed by the Local Chief Executive.
observations.managementAction: The management has implemented the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: The post-audit of agency’s transactions revealed that the management has implemented the recommendations as regards this observation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the management avoid reimbursement as a mode of procurement and adopt public bidding or other alternative modes of procurement provided under RA 9184, in order to promote transparency and to avoid possible source of irregularities or corruption. Likewise, the Management must refrain from using “Certificate of Emergency Purchase” as part of the supporting documents on reimbursement, when the items to be procured has nothing to do during calamities.
observations.managementAction: The management failed to comply with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 3 page 31 of this Annual Audit Report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the BAC and the BAC Secretariat to comply with the requirements of posting of Notices of Award, Notices of Proceed and copies of approved contract in the Philgeps to show full compliance with the principle of transparency as mandated by R.A No. 9184.
observations.reason: The management do not submit the set of documents for auditorial contract review analysis. While there were Notices of Award for some procurements as attached in their submitted disbursement vouchers, the Audit Team could not ascertain the total implementation of the audit recommendation due to the non-submission of the contracts and their supporting documents forming parts thereof. Thus, this audit observation was addressed on Finding No. 6, page 38 of this Annual Audit Report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Budget Office strictly comply with the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 of the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG by avoiding those non-related disaster expenditures to be charged in the LDRRM Fund.
observations.managementAction: The management has compiled with the recommendations.
observations.reason: The report on utilization of LDRRM fund disclosed that the fund was used for disaster related programs/ activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasures transfer the unspent portion of the LDRRMF from General Fund to Trust Fund pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-1. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant shall record the transfer of fund under Trust Fund using the account “Trust Liabilities-DRRM as by PPSAS.
observations.managementAction: The unspent balance for the year under audit were not transferred to the Special Trust Liabilities Account – DRRM.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 13, Page 52 of this Annual Audit Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to render their accounts on time and submit the same for audit in accordance with the existing laws, audit rules and regulations. Non-submission of these paid vouchers may warrant the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance as a result of post-audit.
observations.managementAction: The management failed to implement the audit recommendations.
observations.reason:
The same audit observation was noted for the year under audit. 

This audit observation was reiterated in Finding No. 5, Page 36 of this Annual Audit Report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer shall exert utmost effort to meet the demands of timely reporting and submission of accounts.
