observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that in order to establish an accurate and reliable balance of all municipal properties recorded in the books, management should undertake the following measures:
The Municipal Engineer and the Property Custodian conduct a yearly physical count of local road network projects and consequently update the local road map of the LGU;
The Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian the description and cost segregation of road components of various road infrastructure projects; and
The Municipal Accountant keeps and maintain subsidiary ledger for roads and its components and reconcile the same to the result of the physical at the end of each year.

We have recommended that the following measures be undertaken by management:
The Municipal Mayor instructs the Accountant and Treasurer to enforce the collection of the Loans Receivable.
The Municipal Assessor furnishes the Accountant copy of certified list of taxpayers at the beginning of the year as basis for recording RPT and SET Receivable.
The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer maintain data as to the composition of the uncollected RPT and SET receivables. Issue demand letter if necessary.
Management is advised to exert effort in locating documents pertaining to the receivable accounts. In the event that the supporting documents could no longer be retrieved, a request for write-off could be considered.

In view of the noted discrepancies between the records of the LGU and other government agencies, we have recommended the following:
The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer reconcile their record with the GSIS and retrace transaction from previous year to ascertain the validity of the recorded Other Payables account if possible; and
Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer regularly reconcile their quarterly financial records with the records of other government agencies such as HDMF and GSIS.

In view of the above noted observation, we have recommended that management submit justification on the reimbursements made by the LGU officials and submit additional supporting documents, if any. Henceforth, limit using reimbursement as method of payment for procurement of property, plant and equipment and MOOE unless there are unforeseen events which will justify the immediate payment thereof.
We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor hire qualified personnel for the mandatory positions as provided under R.A. No. 7160 in order to improve the conduct of LGU operations.
We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses. The Municipal Mayor instructs the MPDC to discuss with the LDC the preparation and adoption of both plans for CY 2018 onwards.
We have recommended that management justify the above noted findings. Henceforth, observe the PS limitation in both budget preparation and budget execution phase.

We have recommended that management justify the non-payment of the terminal leave benefits of the remaining four (4) employees. Henceforth, the management is hereby advised to prioritize the payment of terminal leave benefits of the employees for them to enjoy their rightful benefits.

We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses and immediately submit the copies of the contracts for auditorial review and evaluation. Henceforth, adhere to the transparency provisions as provided by the different rules and regulations cited above.

We recommend that the agency submit a copy of Annual Procurement Plan (APP) as well as PMR of the LGU for CY 2017 and henceforth, an APP and PMR should be prepared, approved and submitted to the GPPB.

We have recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee justify the noted lapses and submit the needed documents for verification.  Henceforth, comply with the rules and regulations as required by R.A. No. 9184.

We have recommended that Management justify the noted lapses. Further, we recommend that Management submit the urban/sub-urban greening plan of the Municipality. Henceforth, conduct coordinated tree planting activities and likewise fully utilize the nurseries so as to sustain the NGP project of the Municipality.

In view of the above noted audit issues, we have recommended the following:
The Management submit a copy of a duly approved Local School Board Budget as well as justification as to the reason for the failure to submit said budget on time despite various verbal and written demand made;
Submit the above noted lacking supporting documents in order to determine the validity of the charges made to the fund; and
Henceforth, the LSB Budget should be submitted and made public together with the Annual Budget of the LGU and that charges to be made against the fund should be properly supported with the required certification and documentary requirements.

We have recommended that management make representation with the Bureau of Treasury and/or the Department of Budget and Management as the case maybe to secure a copy of the SARO/NADAI so as to ascertain the purposes of the funds downloaded to the LGU.

In view of the above noted lapses, we have recommended that the following actions be undertaken by management:
The Municipal Mayor instructs the Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to justify the above noted observations.
Observe the doctrine of separation of power between the Executive and Legislative department of the LGU. Henceforth, the respective members of the Sangguniang Bayan should only perform functions as prescribed under Section 445, Article II and 446, Article III of R.A. 7160.
Revisit DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 on the expenditure items not covered or are prohibited to be paid out of the 20% Development Fund for guidance. Henceforth, the Municipal Mayor and the members of the LDC monitor compliance with the above cited regulations.
Justify the purchase of second hand vehicles through a supplier with doubtful qualification and without conducting technical study. Moreover, provide the original documents covering the purchase thereof including the computation of its Residual Useful Life. The submission of the official receipts/ certificate of registration is also hereby asked to be provided by management

We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses. Submit the LCPC Annual Work and Financial Plan, CLJIP as well as SB resolution adopting the specific expenditures allowed from the said fund, if any. Henceforth, management abide with the rules and regulations with regard to the formulation of budget and utilization of the 1% LCPC fund.
We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses. The Municipal Engineer designate is also advised to abide with the provisions of DPWH Department Order No. 11 S. 2014

In view of the noted lapses, we have recommended the following:
The Municipal Mayor creates a the required Gender Responsive Monitoring and Evaluation (M&E) System;
The MSWDO being the leading authority in identifying gender related issues and concerns, see to it that GAD activities are identified through the use of HGDG and GAD Database, that the GAD plan are regularly updated and that the identified projects do address the GAD issues and concern in the long run; and
Henceforth, management should follow the prescribed format of GAD plan and see to it that said plan and budget are approved by the DILG and the PPDO.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Inventory Committee to complete physical inventory which shall covers all municipal properties regardless of date acquired so that the Physical Inventory Report submitted to the Office of the Auditor is accurate and reliable as to whereabouts and conditions of all municipal properties.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide depreciation expense and impairment loss on the following PPE mentioned above in order to present correctly their balances in the financial statements in consonance with PPSAS.

We have recommended that the Petty Cash Custodian refrain from performing collecting functions. Further, the Municipal Treasurer to designate a separate employee to handle the collections to ensure that a sound system of internal control is in place in the Treasury Office.

We have recommended that all Revenue Collection Clerks should remit their daily collections to Municipal Treasurer as required under Section 29 of the Manual of NGAS, Vol. I. Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer should set control measures to avoid or minimize delayed remittance of collections and institute disciplinary actions to concerned accountable employees under her supervision, if necessary.

We have recommended that the Management strictly follow the rules stated in Section 4.1.1 to 4.1.4 of COA Circular 97-002 governing the granting and utilization of cash advance and make sure that all cash advances: (1) Must have Legal Purpose; (2) Previous ones are fully liquidated or settled before another cash advance is granted; (3) Only cash advance for travelling expenses is allowed to Elected Officials, they are not allowed to cash advance for the purpose of procuring equipment and to pay operating expenses of the municipality.

We have recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and Accountant to strictly comply with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 and refrain from those expense items that are not connected with the implementation of development projects, programs and activities of the municipality. The 20% Development Fund shall be appropriated and disbursed only for the very purpose for which the fund was established.

We have recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and Municipal Engineer to properly evaluate the technical viability of the projects included in the list of Local Development Projects and to regularly monitor the status of project implementation so that necessary interventions can be introduced at the earliest time possible time to avoid delays and backlogs. Likewise, the BAC members shall schedule early the procurement activities pertaining to development projects to ensure their timely implementation and completion.

We have recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and Accountant refrain from utilizing appropriated funds under MOOE in the payment of monetization of Leave Credits.  The funds to be utilized in the payment of monetization must come from the appropriation of Terminal Leave/Monetization under PS.  Likewise, the Municipal Budget Officer shall appropriate funds for TLB equivalent to the total earn leave credits of all regular officials and employees of the Municipality.

We have recommended that the Management strictly remit the compulsory premiums on the period stipulated by the above-mentioned law. The remittances should take priority over payment of any and all other obligations, except salaries and wages of employees.

We have recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from certifying as to the availability of funds unless there is existence of an appropriation, and the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the requirements of accrual basis of accounting and the matching principle of expenses to reflect the correct balance of net income for a particular period. Moreover, prior year’s obligations not be charged against current year appropriation unless a supplemental budget was enacted for that purpose.

We have recommended that the management avoid reimbursement as a mode of procurement and adopt public bidding or other alternative modes of procurement provided under RA 9184, in order to promote transparency and to avoid possible source of irregularities or corruption. Likewise, the Management must refrain from using  “Certificate of Emergency Purchase” as part of the supporting documents on reimbursement, when the items to be procured has nothing to do during calamities.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the BAC and the BAC Secretariat to comply with the requirements of posting of Notices of Award, Notices of Proceed and copies of approved contract in the PhilGEPS to show full compliance with the principle of transparency as mandated by R.A No. 9184. Moreover, we advised the BAC Secretariat to attach a Certification in the disbursement voucher stating that he posted the advertisement of the procured project in the conspicuous places within the Municipality.

We have recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer strictly comply with the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 of the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG by avoiding those non-related disaster expenditures to be charged in the LDRRM Fund.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer transfer the unspent portion of the LDRRMF from General Fund to Trust Fund pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-1. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant shall record the transfer of fund under Trust Fund using the account “Trust Liabilities-DRRM as prescribed by PPSAS.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to render their accounts on time and submit the same for audit in accordance with the existing laws, audit rules and regulations. Non-submission of these paid vouchers may warrant the issuance of Notice of Suspension/ Disallowance as a result of post-audit.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer exert utmost effort to meet the demands of timely reporting and submission of accounts.  This is to avoid undue delays in the conduct of post audit and preparing the required reports in compliance with existing laws, rules and regulations.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant follow the correct journal entries involving the set up of RPT/SET Receivables and its corresponding Deferred Income to avoid difference in amounts, since these accounts are considered as reciprocal accounts. Moreover, the Municipal Assessor to furnish the Municipal Accountant a copy of the assessment roll of landowners which will be the basis for the amount due to be recognized as RPT Receivable during the year.

We have recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant to exercise care and diligence in recording and classifying the financial transactions so that the financial statements will not be misleading. In addition, she has to prepare necessary adjusting journal entry to present fairly the correct balances of the accounts.
We have recommended that the Petty Cash Custodian to refund the unexpended year-end balance of the PCF as provided under Section 5.8 of COA Circular No. 97-002.

We reiterate our previous year recommendation for the Municipal Mayor to refrain from reimbursing monthly subscription from Globe and Smart in addition to the monthly load allowance received by him.  The Local Chief Executive shall choose either to claim for load allowance or to reimburse its actual bill from Globe and Smart.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer enforce the collection of the Loans Receivable. Also, exert all efforts necessary in determining the composition of the balance of the Other receivables account.

observations.recommendation:
2.6 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer
enforce the collection of the Loans Receivable. Also, exert all efforts
necessary in determining the composition of the balance of the Other
receivables account.

3.9 In view of the noted discrepancies between the records of the LGU and other
government agencies, we have recommended the following:
 The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer reconcile their record with the
GSIS and retrace transaction from previous year to ascertain the validity of
the recorded Other Payables account if possible;
 The Municipal Accountant adjust the corresponding Due to Pag-Ibig
Account by taking up in the books the proposed adjusting journal entry; and
Description Code Debit Credit
Due to Pag-Ibig 2-02-01-030 ₱ 2,984.02
Prior Period Adjustments 3-01-01-020 ₱ 2,984.02
 Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer regularly reconcile
their quarterly financial records with the records of other government
agencies such as HDMF and GSIS.

4.4 In view of the above noted lapses, we have recommended the following on the
issues with regard to the procurement of medicines:
 That management require the supplier of noted technical requirements;
 That management or the BAC justify the non-submission of BAC
Resolution recommending the method of procurement to be used as well
as justify the awarding of the goods to a supplier with unknown
qualification;
4.5 While the following were recommended on infrastructure projects:
 That the management justify the non-submission of the documents on
time. Likewise, require the Municipal Engineer to submit the needed
documents for verification;
 Submit the supporting documents for the approved work extension order
as well as warranty securities if any; and
 Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer require the
submission of an irrevocable standby letter of credit or surety bond
before the release of the 15% Mobilization Fee and Retention Money.
4.6 Lastly we have recommended that henceforth, management prepare a
checklist of documentary requirements to be included in the disbursement
vouchers. The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer ensure that all
documents were submitted and reviewed before any payment is to be made
and that abide with the rules and regulations with regard to the procurement
of medicines and infrastructure projects.

5.9 In view of the above noted observation, we have recommended that
management submit justification on the reimbursements made by the LGU
officials and submit additional supporting documents, if any. Henceforth,
limit using reimbursement as method of payment for procurement of
property, plant and equipment and MOOE unless there are unforeseen
events which will justify the immediate payment thereof.

6.7 We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor hire qualified personnel
for the mandatory positions as provided under R.A. No. 7160 in order to
improve the conduct of LGU operations.

7.6 We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses. The
Municipal Mayor instructs the MPDC to discuss with the LDC the
preparation and adoption of both plans for CY 2017 onwards.

8.6 We have recommended that management justify the above noted findings.
Henceforth, observe the PS limitation in both budget preparation and budget
execution phase.

9.8 We have recommended that management justify the non-payment of the
terminal leave benefits of the remaining five (5) employees. Henceforth, the
management is hereby advised to prioritize the payment of terminal leave
benefits of the employees for them to enjoy their rightful benefits.

10.6 We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses and
immediately submit the copies of the contracts for auditorial review and
evaluation. Henceforth, adhere to the transparency provisions as provided by
the different rules and regulations cited above

11.6 We have recommended that the agency submit a copy of Annual
Procurement Plan (APP) of the agency for 2016 and henceforth, an APP
should be prepared and approved before any procurement is made.

12.9 In view of the above noted lapses, we have recommended that:
 Management identify the mode of procurement used in the hiring of the
consultant;
 The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer justify the payment of the
transactions despite the absence of the BAC Resolution recommending
the mode of procurement used and award of the contract; and
 The Municipal Mayor justify entering into contract without the
concurrence of the majority of the BAC.

13.13 We have recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee justify the noted
lapses and submit the needed documents for verification. Henceforth, comply
with the rules and regulations as required by R.A. No. 9184.

14.8 We have recommended that Management justify the noted lapses. Also, we
recommended that Management submit the urban/sub-urban greening plan
of the Municipality. Henceforth, conduct coordinated tree planting activities
and likewise fully utilize the nurseries so as to sustain the NGP project of the
Municipality.

15.7 In view of the above noted lapses, we have recommended the following:
 The MPDC sees to it that henceforth all relevant cost for the
implementation of the SWMP is integrated in the Annual Budget as well as
Annual Investment Plan. Coordination among the different offices is hereby
advised;
 The Municipal Mayor convenes the SWMB and seeks justification as to the
reason for the low success rate for the implementation of SWM projects; and
 The members of the SWMB revisit the provisions of the IRR of R.A. No.
9003. Henceforth, implement quality assurance/monitoring mechanism to
facilitate an efficient and effective implementation of the programs
indicated in the SWMP

17.6 We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses. Submit the
required documents and henceforth familiarize themselves and abide with
the rules and regulations set forth by the source agencies.

18.7 We have recommended that management submit justification on the noted
lapses and submit the required supporting documents above mentioned.
Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer ensure that the
required supporting documents are attached to the corresponding DV before
submitting the same to the COA office.

19.8 In view of the foregoing, we have recommended that the management:
a. Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) and the Municipal Planning
and Development Coordinator (MPDC) justify the above noted
observations;
b. Observe the doctrine of separation of power between the Executive and
Legislative department of the LGU. Henceforth, the respective members of
the Sangguniang Bayan should only perform functions as prescribed under
Section 445, Article II and 446, Article III of R.A. 7160; and
c. Revisit DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 on the
expenditure items not covered or are prohibited to be paid out of the 20%
Development Fund for guidance. Henceforth, the Municipal Mayor and the
members of the LDC monitor compliance with the above cited regulations.

20.7 In view of the foregoing observations, we have recommended the following
be complied with:
 The Municipal Mayor and the Sangguniang Bayan Members, thru the
issuance of an SB resolution, create a MDRRMO and provide the
appropriate PS and MOOE fund relative thereto;
 The Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to see to
it that the copy of the LDRRMP should henceforth be attached to the
Annual Budget for review and approval of its respective sanggunian;
 The MDRRMO conduct the necessary VAA and formulate a LCCAP
to be integrated to the LDRRMP and AIP of the LGU;
 The MDRRMO Designate/MSWDO or other personnel in-charge of
the distribution of relief goods submit the required liquidation
documents for audit purposes in order to determine whether the goods
were actually distributed to the intended beneficiaries; and
 The Municipal Budget Officer exercise due diligence in the
computation of the mandatory trust liabilities of the LGU.

21.7 We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses. The
Municipal Engineer designate is also advised to continuously update herself
with the new guidelines for infrastructure projects and henceforth abide with
the provisions of DPWH Department Order No. 11 S. 2014.

23.11 In view of the noted lapses, we have recommended the following:
 The Municipal Mayor creates a the required Gender Responsive Monitoring
and Evaluation (M&E) System;
 The Municipal Mayor with the help of the Sangguniang Bayan, formulate and
adapt a Local GAD Code;
 The MSWDO being the leading authority in identifying gender related issues
and concerns, see to it that GAD activities are identified through the use of
HGDG and GAD Database, that the GAD plan are regularly updated and that
the identified projects do address the GAD issues and concern in the long run;
and
 Henceforth, management should follow the prescribed format of GAD plan
and see to it that said plan and budget are approved by the DILG and the
PPDO.

25. We have recommended that Management require the persons responsible for the
audit suspensions to settle their account.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive create an inventory committee to conduct physical inventory of all property of the LGU and submit a Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) pursuant to existing auditing rules and regulations mentioned above. Thereafter, require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the book records with the inventory report.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide depreciation expense and impairment loss on the following PPE mentioned above in order to correctly present their balances in the financial statements in consonance with PPSAS.
Furthermore, the Local Chief Executive to create an appraisal committee task to determine the useful life and appraise value of the above PPE as a basis for the computation of depreciation expense and impairment loss by the Municipal Accountant.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer require all Revenue Collecting Personnel to remit their collections at the close of each business day pursuant to provisions of Section 29 of the Manual of NGAS, Volume I. Furthermore, collections coming from Real Property Tax must be closely and stringently monitored to avoid late remittance which could possibly result to misuse or loss of public funds.

We have recommended the payrolls of regular personnel be prepared and processed on a monthly basis. The payroll should be prepared in two (2) sets, reflecting both the 1st and 2nd quicena of the month. The net pay should be arrived at by adding the basic salary and all allowances less total deductions divided by two (2).
Likewise, the HRMO shall prepare the Monthly Payroll of the officers and employees of the municipality in order to unload unrelated work from the LDRRM Office and to promote proper segregation of duties and operational efficiency.

We have recommended that the Human Resource Management Officer and the Municipal Accountant not to allow municipal officials and employees to avail the monetization using the balance of their sick leave credits, in the absence of any proof of financial difficulty experienced by them at the time of their application pursuant to Section 23 of the Omnibus Rules on Leave.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant make a thorough review of the Itinerary of Travel for every official and employee claiming travelling expenses and per diems chargeable against the funds of the municipality. The Municipal Accountant must see to it that the rates provided under E.O No. 298 are strictly followed. The audit team is expecting that this incorrect practice should stop or otherwise it would force us to disallow in audit all succeeding travelling expenses and per Diems which in our belief is considered Excessive.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the adjusting journal entry to capitalized the following expenditures as Inventory, Property, Plant and Equipment, and Intangible Asset to reflect the correct balance of those accounts in line with PPSAS No. 12 17 and 31, as well as to  maintain the proper accountability of those assets.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant be strict in the granting of Cash Advance and observe those allowable transactions that can only be availed of thru cash advance pursuant to COA Circular 97-002.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant examine the completeness of the supporting documents for every transactions of the Municipality before approving the same. If there are claims submitted to the Accounting Office for processing, but lacks the required supporting documents, return the DV to the claimant for proper documentation.
Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer shall pay only claims that are properly approved/certified and supported with complete documentation in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly review the disbursement vouchers relating to the payment of Gasoline Expenses and not to allow those issued Drivers Trip Tickets that were utilized for private purposes. Likewise, the person in charge in the issuance of Drivers Trip Ticket shall scrutinize the intention of those official and employees asking for Gas Slip and shall be supported by a Travel Order duly signed by the Local Chief Executive.

We have recommended that the Management refrain from paying additional Monthly Subscription from Telecommunication Companies (TELCOS) in addition to the Monthly Load Allowance received by Elected and Department Officials of the Municipality.  The officials concerned must choose either to claim for Load Allowance or to reimburse their actual bill from these TELCOS.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer exert utmost efforts to meet the demands of timely reporting and submission of accounts.  This is to avoid undue delays that preclude the timely conduct of audit.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor as the Head of the Procurement Entity observe strictly the pertinent provisions of RA 9184 on the procurement of goods and services which should be coursed thru BAC and with public bidding as the primary mode, to promote accountability, economy and transparency in the procurement process.

We have recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer refrain from accommodating expenditures even in the absence sufficient income from SEF. Moreover, the Municipal Treasurer is encouraged to intensify their collections in SEF to meet the budgeted expenditures of the municipality and to maximize the benefits that the students and teachers could avail under this fund.
We have recommended that the proposed adjusting entry be taken up in the books of accounts
